text: The patient is positioned in the prone jack-knife position for the procedure. A dorsal perineal block is administered with injections of lidocaine and bupivacaine at multiple sites. An Onsler anoscope is inserted to provide access to the surgical area.

The surgeon retracts the hemorrhoid at the three o'clock position into the lumen using forceps and performs a V-shaped excision up to two centimeters above the dentate line. Continuous sutures are applied to ligate the vascular pedicle up to the perineal skin. The same procedure is repeated at the eleven o'clock position. Coagulation and suture pexy are performed at the seven o'clock position.
A hemostatic sponge is placed intra-anally to control bleeding following the excision and suturing of hemorrhoids.
